FALL ARMYWORM.current_treatment: Practice regular farm sanitation including weeding. Handpick and destroy egg masses and larvae found in the leaf whorl early in the morning. Apply Bacillus thuringiensis (Bypel) at 15g per 15 liters of water using a knapsack sprayer, preferably early morning or late evening when larvae are most active. For severe infestations, alternate with Lambda-cyhalothrin (Warrior Super) at 40ml per 15 liters every two weeks to prevent resistance. Monitor your farm regularly to detect infestations early and make informed control decisions.
FALL ARMYWORM.prevention: Plant early at the onset of the rainy season to avoid peak infestation periods. Use certified seeds from approved sources. Practice intensive spraying two weeks after sowing for early control. Intercrop maize with cassava to encourage biological control through natural predators. Apply nitrogenous fertilizer early in the first week after sowing to promote vigorous growth that can withstand pest attack. Practice crop rotation with groundnut or other non-host crops. Remove and burn all crop debris after harvesting to eliminate breeding sites. Always inquire about the pest history of the land before planting. Monitor the farm regularly for early problem detection and timely insect control.

MAIZE WEEVIL.current_treatment: -Keep the farm and surroundings clean clean -Avoid late planting or off-season planting and avoid planting new crop near infested fields -Use a mixture of maize varieties when planting; varieties showing resistance/tolerance, short duration or increased leaf toughness.
MAIZE WEEVIL.prevention: -Monitor 10 consecutive plants in 10 locations of the field -Look for creamy/grey egg masses covered in silk located on the underside or top of the leaves and on the stems -Look for light green/dark brown caterpillars with longitudinal .

FUSARIUM WILT.current_treatment: Fusarium wilt of tomatoes - Tomatoes -Rogue out and destroy plants that show early symptoms of the disease and destroy by burning.
FUSARIUM WILT.prevention: Fusarium wilt of tomatoes - Tomatoes -Extract seeds from healthy fruits - Certified seeds from recognised institution or MoFA. Avoid planting on land with history of wilt disease -Rotate nursery site each season and maintain good drainage -Destroy plant debris immediately after harvest by burning -Practise long crop rotation - 6 years with non-solanaceous plants (such as cereals and grains- maize, millet) after every 3 seasons

CASSAVA MOSAIC VIRUS.current_treatment: Remove and immediately destroy severely infected plants by burning. Do not use infected cuttings for propagation under any circumstances. Control whitefly vectors (which spread the virus) by spraying with Cypadem 43.6% EC at 50ml per 16 liters of water or use imidacloprid at 30ml per 15 liters. Apply early in the morning or late evening, ensuring thorough coverage of leaf undersides where whiteflies congregate.
CASSAVA MOSAIC VIRUS.prevention: Plant improved virus-resistant varieties such as Bankyehemaa, Abasafitaa, Ampong, or Esambankye. Use only healthy, disease-free cuttings from certified sources. Carefully inspect all planting materials before use and reject any showing mosaic symptoms. Rotate cassava with maize for 2-3 years to break the disease cycle. Remove and destroy volunteer cassava plants around fields as these harbor the virus. Control whitefly populations through regular monitoring and timely intervention.

DAMPING OFF.current_treatment: Use certified Tomato seed; Practise crop rotation; Practice Farm hygiene; Use Shavit F
DAMPING OFF.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

WHITEFLIES.current_treatment: Spray Cypadem 43.6% EC at 50mls/16lts of water; Rotate cassava with maize for 3 years
WHITEFLIES.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

BACTERIAL WILT.current_treatment: Use cultivars resistance to bacterial wilt.Avoid the cultivation of host plant such as.potato ,tobacco and egg plants on the field and around.Avoid planting tomato on infested fields.Practise crop rotation with nonsusceptiple crops if you need to cutivate the field.
BACTERIAL WILT.prevention: Use only healthy, disease-free planting materials from certified sources. Practice strict field sanitation by disinfecting tools between plants. Avoid planting in fields with disease history for at least 4-5 years. Ensure good drainage and avoid waterlogging. Do not work in fields when plants are wet. Remove volunteer plants that may harbor bacteria.

CATERPILLAR.current_treatment: Apply Lamda at 40ml/ 15 litres of water.
CATERPILLAR.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

COCOA STEM BORER.current_treatment: Cocoa stem borers - COCOA -Plug bore holes with a stick to stop adult emerging -Remove and burn all heavily infested branches. Copice trees with higher infestation and cut trees with infestation low in their stem. If holes extend into the roots Remove roots that have holes in them as well.
COCOA STEM BORER.prevention: Cocoa stem borers - COCOA -Select and use only borer-free seedlings and young plants for planting -Use none hosts plants for fruit and shade trees when establishing cocoa fields. -Uproot and completely destroy damaged trees which serve as diapausing sites -Monitoring should be carried out throughout the year on aweekly basis -Inspect tree stems for entry holes and check for frass at the base of the tree or gum oozing out of the bore holes. Also look for discolouration on stems and shoots -If in doubt, plants showing discolouration should be cut open to observe a white larva with a brown head and tunnels in the stem or branches -When any bore holes are observed carry out control measures

APHID.current_treatment: Insecticides like Acetastar, cydim super and cymethoate super can be use at a rate of 50mls/knapsack to control the insects.; Use of soap solution (Alata soap) to control the aphids is also recommended.
APHID.prevention: Monitor fields regularly, at least twice weekly, for early pest detection. Use integrated pest management combining cultural, biological, and chemical controls. Maintain field sanitation by removing crop debris. Plant early to avoid peak pest populations. Use resistant varieties where available. Encourage natural predators by avoiding broad-spectrum insecticides.

CASSAVA MEALYBUG.current_treatment: Good farm sanitation; Spray with Acetastar at a rate of 50mls/16lts of water
CASSAVA MEALYBUG.prevention: Monitor fields regularly, at least twice weekly, for early pest detection. Use integrated pest management combining cultural, biological, and chemical controls. Maintain field sanitation by removing crop debris. Plant early to avoid peak pest populations. Use resistant varieties where available. Encourage natural predators by avoiding broad-spectrum insecticides.

BACTERIAL SPOT.current_treatment: Remove affected plants from the farm; Crop rotation with garden eggs for 2years; Resistant/Tolerant varieties should be planted
BACTERIAL SPOT.prevention: Use only healthy, disease-free planting materials from certified sources. Practice strict field sanitation by disinfecting tools between plants. Avoid planting in fields with disease history for at least 4-5 years. Ensure good drainage and avoid waterlogging. Do not work in fields when plants are wet. Remove volunteer plants that may harbor bacteria.

SOFT ROT.current_treatment: Rotate crops with non-host crops and apply NPK 15 15 15 at a rate of 20g/stand; You can also apply poultry manure.
SOFT ROT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

CASSAVA ROOT ROT.current_treatment: Adopt crop rotation; Monitor the problem and acquire resistant varities if you want to use the same piece of land; Do not use any insecticide since the cause of the problem is not an insect.
CASSAVA ROOT ROT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

BLOSSOM END ROT.current_treatment: Blossom End Rot - TOMATOES Remove affected fruits to avoid secondary infection. Spray entire plants with Technokel Calcium @40 mls /15litre knapsack. Observe PHI of 7 days.
BLOSSOM END ROT.prevention: Blossom End Rot - TOMATOES Avoid excessive application of nitrogen fertiliser. Mulch ridges or beds .Apply sufficient amount of water.

LEAF SPOT.current_treatment: Crop rotation; Field sanitation and use of resistant varieties; Spray Carbendazim at a rate of 30cc to 15 lit of water; Top cop is also recommended to spray at fortnight interval
LEAF SPOT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

DROUGHT/WATER STRESS.current_treatment: Use stress resistant variety. Practice irrigation. Rotate crops with non-host crops.
DROUGHT/WATER STRESS.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

NUTRIENT DEFICIENCY.current_treatment: Practice farm Sanitation; Use Best Fertilizer at one tin tomato full to 15 L of water
NUTRIENT DEFICIENCY.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

MAIZE STEM BORER.current_treatment: Control weeds properly. Spray lambda at 30ml/15L of water into sides and centtral whorl of plant. Destroy all stubbles after harvest before next planting
MAIZE STEM BORER.prevention: Monitor fields regularly, at least twice weekly, for early pest detection. Use integrated pest management combining cultural, biological, and chemical controls. Maintain field sanitation by removing crop debris. Plant early to avoid peak pest populations. Use resistant varieties where available. Encourage natural predators by avoiding broad-spectrum insecticides.

ANTHRACNOSE.current_treatment: For Antracnose on TOMATO, Remove all diseased plants and destroy
ANTHRACNOSE.prevention: For Antracnose on TOMATO, Stake and mulching Crop rotation with non solanaceau crops Spray using mancozeb at100g/15 l of water

BORERS.current_treatment: Manually collect and destroy affected fruits
BORERS.prevention: Spray Bypel at 20g in 15 l of water every 2 weeks after transplanting.Avoid excessive use of nitrogen fertiliser

AFLATOXIN.current_treatment: For Mould on MAIZE, Shell immediately, sort and discard affected grains.Do not feed to animals.
AFLATOXIN.prevention: For Mould on MAIZE, Harvest the maize soon after ripening when all leaves are dry and cobs turned down. Store in well ventilated place

SPIDER MITES.current_treatment: Tomato spider mite Spray with local soap at 150g in 15L of water.
SPIDER MITES.prevention: Tomato spider mite Do not grow tomato after host plants like Peppers. Okro etc Apply Sulphur 80WP at 50g in l5l of water at early detection.

LEAF CURL VIRUS.current_treatment: LEAF CURL - TOMATOES Imidacloprid such as. Punto should be sprayed on the entire plant and below the leaves; eggs and flies are often found below the leaves. Spray every 14-21 days and rotate on a monthly basis with Abamectin such as. Abamet at 2 0-40 ml/16 litres of water and Imidacloprid such as.Punto at (20 - 30 )mls in 16 Litres of water so that the whiteflies do not build-up resistance to chemicals.Re - entry interval for both pesticides is 24 hours and pre- harvest interval is 3 days.
LEAF CURL VIRUS.prevention: LEAF CURL - TOMATOES Cultural control: Fields should be inspected daily for the presence of whiteflies in all crops, not just tomato plants as the whitefly may have passed the virus onto another crop.Plant maize around the field to reduce the ability of the whiteflies to reach the crop. Water with any recommended compound fertilizer to reduce stress.

EARLY BLIGHT.current_treatment: Remove and burn infected plants detected early to prevent spread. Transport diseased materials away from the field in a closed container. Apply Mancozeb fungicide at 24-32g per 15-liter knapsack at 10-day intervals. Ensure thorough coverage of all plant surfaces including undersides of leaves. Observe the pre-harvest interval of 7 days before harvesting treated crops.
EARLY BLIGHT.prevention: Use disease-free or certified seeds for planting. Rotate tomato with maize or legumes every two seasons to break disease cycles. Practice staking and mulching to prevent fruit from touching the soil and improve air circulation. Remove and destroy all crop debris after harvest and eliminate solanaceous weeds that may act as disease hosts. Promote good air circulation by maintaining proper spacing of 70-80cm between rows and 40-60cm between plants. Monitor fields regularly for lesions with chlorotic margins on leaves, dark circular spots with concentric zones, or black sunken lesions on fruits. Apply preventive fungicides early in the season or when first symptoms appear.

ABIOTIC.current_treatment: Adviced to use carbadism to spray to control fungi infection on Tomato seedlings.
ABIOTIC.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

FLOWER/FRUIT ABORTION.current_treatment: Spray K Optimal at 40ml/15lit of water.
FLOWER/FRUIT ABORTION.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

FRUIT FLIES.current_treatment: Fruit bores - TOMATOES Manually collect and destroy affected fruits. Spray EMAMECTIN BENZOATE such as Attack 1.9 EC at 40 ml in 15L of water.
FRUIT FLIES.prevention: Fruit bores - TOMATOES Remove and destroy infected fruit and infested plants after harvest. Rotate crops with non-host crops with non Solanaceau crops. Apply Bacillus thurengiensis such as BYPEL at 20g in 15l of water after transplanting.

FUNGAL DISEASE.current_treatment: Mosaic virus - TOMATOES, practice regular farm sanitation such as weeding. Uproot all diseased plants from the farm and burn. Spray with Golan at 20 ml in 12 litres of water using motorised sprayer to control the white flies. Alternate with Alata soap solution and neem leaves extract plus powdered hot pepper put together at two weeks intervals. Monitor problem early to inform decision of the insects control.
FUNGAL DISEASE.prevention: Mosaic virus - TOMATOES, practice regular farm sanitation such as weeding.Use certified seeds to prepare the nursery. Remove all volunteers plants before you transplant seedlings. Inspect the field regularly regularly to inform you decision of early detection and control of the vector. Spray with neem leaves extract plus powdered hot pepper put together with Alata soap solution of 120-200ml in to 15 litres of water .

MILLIPEDES.current_treatment: Garden Centipedes - CASSAVA - Apply Fipronil (Fipro 50 EC) at the rate of 100mls/15L. Tank sprayer.
MILLIPEDES.prevention: Garden Centipedes - CASSAVA -Avoid Centipedes-infested fields when selecting sites for field establisment -Practice farm sanitation. Collect, burn, bury or compost plant debris. -Avoid mulching in sites with potential Centipedes infestation

LATE BLIGHT.current_treatment: Elimiate all susceptible crops such as.egg plant and potato.Crop resistance varieties in your next planting.Always spray with top cop or Mancozeb forth nightty soon after your establish your tomato field.Top cop Rate 50gm/16lo of water.Mancozeb Rate 50gm/15 of water.
LATE BLIGHT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

LEAF MINER.current_treatment: For leaf miner on TOMATO, use Remon 25mls / 15 liters of water
LEAF MINER.prevention: For leaf miner on TOMATO, rotate crops with non-host crops

MAIZE STREAK VIRUS.current_treatment: Remove all streaked/diseased plants, dry and burn or feed to livestock if applicable. Grow resistant variety in the next growing season.
MAIZE STREAK VIRUS.prevention: Plant virus-resistant varieties where available. Use certified virus-free planting materials. Control insect vectors through regular monitoring and appropriate management. Remove infected plants immediately to prevent spread. Avoid planting near infected fields. Use reflective mulches to repel insect vectors.

BIRDS.current_treatment: Mammals Attack - MAIZE Trap rudent using mouse trap - Remove all hiding places where rudent hide from the
BIRDS.prevention: Mammals Attack - MAIZE Early planting Rotate crops with non-host crops Use seed dresses on the seeds before planting

MOULD.current_treatment: Mould - MAIZE Quickly remove weevils from grains by seivinng,and dispose off immediately.
MOULD.prevention: Mould - MAIZE Harvest maize as soon as it has reached maturity. Remove and burn infested maize residues after harvest.In barn after harvest ,put fresh neem leaves in layers over Grains.

NEMATODES.current_treatment: For nematode control: Tomato, -sterilisation seed beds before planting -Use Manozeb to water the seedlings immediately germination -Before transplanting of seedling soke seedlings in mancozeb
NEMATODES.prevention: For nematode control on tomato: rotate crops with non-host crops Practice land rotation

STINK BUG.current_treatment: Fall Armyworm infestation - Maize 1. Handpick the caterpillars, egg masses and destroy. 2. Spray Agoo @ 50gms / 15L of water unto the funnel of the plant during the early hours of the day. 3. Apply fertilizer for the plant to out grow the damage. 4. Regularly control weeds in and around the cropped field.
STINK BUG.prevention: Fall Armyworm infestatin - Maize 1. Practice Crop Rotation with non - host plants. 2. Plant early in order to escape high pest incidence. 3. Plant in strips by alternating maize rows with such as spring onions or cassava. 4. Burn crop residue after harvest.

RODENTS.current_treatment: Treatment of seeds before planting in the future with a seed dresser and also ensure the clearing of farm boundaries
RODENTS.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

CHEMICAL DAMAGE.current_treatment: Apply Lambda at 40ml per 15 litres of water when maize is about 2 weeks old. Practise good farm hygiene.
CHEMICAL DAMAGE.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

SMUT.current_treatment: MAIZEPromptly remove and destroy (but dont bury) smutted tassels, ears, and infected plants before the fungus spreads, to reduce the build-up of the disease in the soil
SMUT.prevention: MAIZE Use healthy seeds -Use resistant varieties -The fungus survives in soil for several years and infects plants during seedling stage. Use cultural practices that promote rapid seedling germination and emergence: Frequent irrigation for 3 weeks after planting reduces the incidence of the disease Balanced soil fertility. The disease is more severe under nitrogen deficiency -Practise deep ploughing -Practise crop rotation

GRASSHOPPER.current_treatment: Grasshoppers - Maize Spray with Lamda at 40mls per 15litres of water.Repeat after 2weeks if symptoms persist.
GRASSHOPPER.prevention: Grasshoppers - Maize Spray with Karate at 20mls per 15L knapsack when crop is about 2 weeks old.Repeat after 2weeks if symptoms persist.

CASSAVA BACTERIAL BLIGHT.current_treatment: CASSAVA Rogue and burn infected plants that have just appeared to prevent spread -At the first sign of the disease immediately harvest mature roots and destroy all remaining plants and plant debris by burning
CASSAVA BACTERIAL BLIGHT.prevention: CASSAVA The disease is transmitted through stem cuttings, use healthy planting materials obtained from plants without symptoms -Plant resistant varieties such as Bankyehemaa and Esam bankye. -Disinfect farming tools in bleach (5 parts of bleach to 95 parts of water) -Remove old cassava plant debris from previous plantings and burn after harvest -Avoid planting on land with history of CBB. Fallow previous fields for a period of 3-5 years if pressure on land is low or rotate cassava with legume or grains -Avoid transferring planting materials from CBB endemic areas to new cassava fields

COCOA PEST.current_treatment: Apply Akate Master at 100mls to 12 litres of water. Wear protective clothings. Do not eat, drink or smoke during spraying. Thoroughly wash body after spraying.
COCOA PEST.prevention: Ensure proper farm sanitation. Apply Akate Master at 100mls to 12 litres of water at 4 weekly intervals starting August to December excluding November. Wear protective clothings. Do not eat, drink or smoke during spraying. Thoroughly wash body after spraying.

SEED PROBLEM.current_treatment: Acquire seed from registered and certified shops.
SEED PROBLEM.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

BUCKEYE ROT.current_treatment: Plant certified disease free seeds. Practise crop rotation. Stake the tomatoes. If all fail spray any recommended fungicide like Metalaxyl M or Copper oxychloride at their recommended rates.
BUCKEYE ROT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

BOLLWORM.current_treatment: Remove and destroy affected fruits by burying 3 feet deep. Apply Bipel at 15 grams to 15 litres of water. Wear protective clothing. Do not eat, drink or smoke during spraying. Thoroughly wash body after spraying.
BOLLWORM.prevention: Ensure proper farm sanitation. Plant 2 rows of maize plants as barrier 2 weeks before transplanting of seedlings. Apply Bipel at 15 grams to 15 litres of water when 1% of Fruits show signs of infection. Wear protective clothing. Do not eat, drink or smoke during spraying.

LARGER GRAIN BORER.current_treatment: Remove the infested one Keep watch during drying Treat with recommended insecticide
LARGER GRAIN BORER.prevention: Harvest early Burn the infested residue

MOSAIC VIRUS.current_treatment: 1.RESISTANCE VARIETIES SHOULD BE PLANTED 2.REMOVE INFESTED PLANT ESPERCAILLY AT THE NURSERY STAGE 3FIELD HYGEINE SHOULD BE PRACTICE
MOSAIC VIRUS.prevention: Plant virus-resistant varieties where available. Use certified virus-free planting materials. Control insect vectors through regular monitoring and appropriate management. Remove infected plants immediately to prevent spread. Avoid planting near infected fields. Use reflective mulches to repel insect vectors.

MEALYBUG.current_treatment: Weed farm thoughrouly Mix 5gm of alata soap plus 5gm powdered pepper in l Lt of waterabout 30 mins mix the mixture with 15lt of water to spr
MEALYBUG.prevention: Avoid disease d planting materials Fallow land with plantain and maize Practice early planting

CERCOSPORA LEAF SPOT.current_treatment: Use topsin M at 100gm per knapsack sprayer and Repeat at 7 days
CERCOSPORA LEAF SPOT.prevention: Apply recommended Seeds Rotate crops with non-host crops The farm should be weed free Fungicide Treatment should be early to prevent the attack

CASSAVA GREEN MITE.current_treatment: Apply Neem leaves at 1kg/3inch to knapsack onto the plant.Practice farm hygiene
CASSAVA GREEN MITE.prevention: Monitor fields regularly, at least twice weekly, for early pest detection. Use integrated pest management combining cultural, biological, and chemical controls. Maintain field sanitation by removing crop debris. Plant early to avoid peak pest populations. Use resistant varieties where available. Encourage natural predators by avoiding broad-spectrum insecticides.

PHOSPHORUS DEFICIENCY.current_treatment: Farmer advised to apply N.P.K 15;15:15 fertilizer in subsequent planting; Not to waste time and money on already matured plants.
PHOSPHORUS DEFICIENCY.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

LEAFHOPPER.current_treatment: Rotate crops with non-host crops; apply insecticides like Cymethoate at a rate of 30 50mls/15lts of water once a month.
LEAFHOPPER.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

BACTERIAL INFECTION.current_treatment: Selection of plant free from the disease will provide clean planting material to limit CBB spread; Farm sanitation by burning of old infested cassava stems and debris can also reduce CBB spread by rain splashes
BACTERIAL INFECTION.prevention: Use only healthy, disease-free planting materials from certified sources. Practice strict field sanitation by disinfecting tools between plants. Avoid planting in fields with disease history for at least 4-5 years. Ensure good drainage and avoid waterlogging. Do not work in fields when plants are wet. Remove volunteer plants that may harbor bacteria.

PLANTAIN WEEVIL.current_treatment: Good farm sanitation practice, Use banana split stem to trap weevils, collect young plantain and cut into pieces, lay the pieces around the plant with the cut end downward, inspect every morning, kill and destroy the pests, continue the process every day to reduce pests population.
PLANTAIN WEEVIL.prevention: Monitor fields regularly, at least twice weekly, for early pest detection. Use integrated pest management combining cultural, biological, and chemical controls. Maintain field sanitation by removing crop debris. Plant early to avoid peak pest populations. Use resistant varieties where available. Encourage natural predators by avoiding broad-spectrum insecticides.

FRUIT ROT.current_treatment: Farmer was advised to continue using fungicide but this time appropriately. Field visit arranged.
FRUIT ROT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

PHYTOPHTHORA.current_treatment: Use host resistant varieties, buy seeds from certified shops
PHYTOPHTHORA.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

MOTH.current_treatment: Spray with Lamda cyhalothrin at 40ml/ 15 litres of water.
MOTH.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

INSECTS.current_treatment: Remove the affected plants and destroy; Application of insecticides such as Dursban 4E is also recommended since termite attack is suspected.
INSECTS.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

CASSAVA BROWN STREAK DISEASE.current_treatment: Removalof affected plant from the field
CASSAVA BROWN STREAK DISEASE.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

SEPTORIA LEAF SPOT.current_treatment: Burn all tomato remains after harvesting. Practice of crop rotation with cereal and legumes
SEPTORIA LEAF SPOT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

RUST.current_treatment: Termites - YAMS Apply Deltamethrin based product (such as Deltacan at 175-100mls/L. Restrictions:Maximum 2 applications per season in the morning and later in the day. PHI 14 days. REI 24 hours. Eye and skin irritant. Highly toxic to bees and other non target arthropods.Toxic to aquatic organisms. Avoid using near water ways
RUST.prevention: Termites - YAMS Avoid termite-infested fields when selecting sites for field establisment -Destroy termite hill in and around production site before planting -Keep soil moist during the dry season distrupt termite activities -Practice farm sanitation. Collect, burn, bury or compost plant debris.

NITROGEN DEFICIENCY.current_treatment: Fall armyworm - MAIZE Fall armyworm - MAIZE Look for patches of small shot holes "window pane" to large ragged and elongated holes in the leaves emerging from the whorl -&nbsp
NITROGEN DEFICIENCY.prevention: Fall armyworm - MAIZEAvoid late planting. Plant early to avoid peak immigration of adults -&nbsp

SCLEROTIUM WILT.current_treatment: APPLY FUNGICIDE; ROGUE AND BURN INFECTED PLANTS.
SCLEROTIUM WILT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

LEAF BLIGHT.current_treatment: MAIZE -Use resistant and tolerant varieties -Practise deep ploughing of crop residues before planting -Practise crop rotation -Remove and destroy the alternative host
LEAF BLIGHT.prevention: MAIZE Rotate crops with non-host crops

TOMATO LEAF SPOT.current_treatment: TOMATOES Monitor field early for symptoms Transplant only healthy seedlings to new fields and site nurseries away from tomato fields to reduce seedling infections Control weeds on the fields, Mulch field with dry grass to prevent pupation of vectors Spray alpha-cypermethrin at 7.5ml/15L of water to control the vector population.
TOMATO LEAF SPOT.prevention: TOMATOES Transplant only healthy seedlings to new fields

CRICKETS.current_treatment: MAIZE Avoid late planting,plough land before planting,monitor rainfall pattern before sowing and dress seeds with insecticide before sowing Allow natural enemies to feed on the insects.
CRICKETS.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

TOMATO LEAF CURL VIRUS.current_treatment: TOMATOES Remove tomato residues and volunteer crops and bury or burn, Transplant shortly after or during the rainy season, Conserve natural enemies, Keep the field weed-free throughout the cropping season, Spray liquid soap at rate 45 table spoons per 15lit of water to control vectors
TOMATO LEAF CURL VIRUS.prevention: TOMATOES Remove tomato residues and volunteer crops and bury or burn, Transplant shortly after or during the rainy season, Conserve natural enemies, Keep the field weed-free throughout the cropping season,

GROWTH CRACKING.current_treatment: TOMATOES -Heavy rainfall or irrigation following a long, dry period promotes rapid growth during ripening, therefore reduce water supply to the plant. Fruit exposed to direct sun are most susceptible therefore a simple shade can be provided.
GROWTH CRACKING.prevention: TOMATOES - Use plant crack-resistant varieties such as Jetstar. Mulch and water the plants during dry season. Fruit cracking is most common on the large-fruited varieties such as Beefsteak.

MILDEW.current_treatment: ENSURE FIELD SANITATION TO PREVENT THE SPREAD OF THE DISEASES SPRAY WITH 20g OF SULPHUR 80W TO 15L OF WATER AT 7 10 DAYS INTERVALS
MILDEW.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

CUTWORM.current_treatment: PLOUGH AND HARROWING OF THE SOIL EXPOSE THE INSECTS TO THEIR PREDATORS SPRAY 10MLS OF EMAMECTIN WITH 15L OF WATER AT 7 10DAYS INTERVELS, PREFERABLY IN THE EVENING
CUTWORM.prevention: Monitor fields regularly, at least twice weekly, for early pest detection. Use integrated pest management combining cultural, biological, and chemical controls. Maintain field sanitation by removing crop debris. Plant early to avoid peak pest populations. Use resistant varieties where available. Encourage natural predators by avoiding broad-spectrum insecticides.

FRUIT WORM.current_treatment: Fruit worm - Tomatoes - Hand pick and destroy caterpilars which are usually found underside leaves at initial (lower) infestations - Add 1 sachet of Porselen (Emamectin benzoate product)to 15litres of water and spray at 10- 14days interval
FRUIT WORM.prevention: Fruit worm - Tomatoes - Dispose off crop residues: uproot and burn tomato stalks after harvest -Crop rotate or intercrop with non-host crops (e.g onion, carrot) to reduce field.

FRUIT BORER.current_treatment: Fruit borer TOMATOES -Spray one of the following chemicals 2-3 times with intervals of 10-15 days: 1 Sachet of Porselen or 30mls of Eforia insectides to 15L of water.
FRUIT BORER.prevention: Fruit borer TOMATOES -Deep plough the field after harvesting to expose the pupae to the sun and to birds that will feed on them -Predatory ants, spiders, parasitic wasps and parasitic flies are all natural enemies of the fruit borer. Do not kill them if you see them! -Manually collect and destroy affected fruits -When using a pesticide, always wear protective clothing and follow the instructions on the product label, such as dosage, timing of application, and pre-harvest interval.

BLACK POD DISEASE.current_treatment: Black pod - COCOA Remove chupons and reduce heavy canopy to admit light. Remove diseased pods from the farm to an open space and either burn or bury them. Spray with 1 sachet of redomil to 15litres of water using a motorised sprayer
BLACK POD DISEASE.prevention: Black pod -COCOA Reduction of shade trees. Mummified pods should be removed and destroyed to reduce spread Draining standing waters. Regular harvesting. Remove chupons and reduce heavy canopy to admit light. Remove diseased pods from the farm to an open space and either burn or bury them.

SEED ROT.current_treatment: Purchase seeds from recommended source for your subsequent farming. Conduct germination test before planting
SEED ROT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

COWPEA WEEVIL.current_treatment: Cowpea Weevils - MAIZE Thresh cowpea and store seeds in PICS bags
COWPEA WEEVIL.prevention: Cowpea Weevils - MAIZE Harvest matured pods early to avoid infes

DEFORMATION.current_treatment: On MAIZE, 1. Uproot affected plants and feed to livestock
DEFORMATION.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

EGGPLANT FRUIT AND SHOOT BORER.current_treatment: Practice good farm hygine apply redomil to the crops
EGGPLANT FRUIT AND SHOOT BORER.prevention: Monitor fields regularly, at least twice weekly, for early pest detection. Use integrated pest management combining cultural, biological, and chemical controls. Maintain field sanitation by removing crop debris. Plant early to avoid peak pest populations. Use resistant varieties where available. Encourage natural predators by avoiding broad-spectrum insecticides.

BEETLE.current_treatment: Fall Army worms - maize ,Spray with Lambda Cyhalothrin plus By pelvic at the recommended rate to control the insects. Repeat after two weeks and monitor their control after spraying.
BEETLE.prevention: Fall Army worms - maize , Do not cultivate on infested area. Inspect the field regularly early after germination to inform decision of early control of the insects.

TOMATO SPOTTED WILT VIRUS.current_treatment: Spoted wilt virus - TOMATOES Spray field with imidacloprid at 5-7ml/15L of water to control thrip and other insect vectors, if vector populations persist. A systemic neonicotinoid insecticide,
TOMATO SPOTTED WILT VIRUS.prevention: Spoted wilt virus - TOMATOES Transplant only healthy seedlings to new fields and site nurseries away from tomato fields to reduce seedling infections -Thrips are the main vector in the transmission of TSWV, in nurseries plants can be covered with with a fine mesh (sizes smaller than 0.5mm) to prevent access to the thrips.

ANGULAR LEAF SPOT.current_treatment: Maintain proper farm sanitation such as regular weeding. Remove affected fruits and destroy them outside farm. Apply effective fungicide such as Top Cop at 60ml per knapsack. Spray every 2 weeks interval for 7 months from emergence of new leaves to beginning of flowering.
ANGULAR LEAF SPOT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

ROOT ROT.current_treatment: Fungal root Rot - CASSAVA early harvesting of crops.
ROOT ROT.prevention: Fungal root Rot - CASSAVA Avoid water logged areas and plant disease free and tolerrant varieties. Also ensure GAPS in Cassava production.

NECROSIS.current_treatment: Use diseases free planting material. Plant resistant varieties like Ampon,Oburoni,etc. Maintain farm sanitation. Avoiding transporting diseases or affected planting material from one field to another. Rotate crops with non-host crops.
NECROSIS.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

RICE BLAST.current_treatment: Blight disease - TOMATOES removal of affected plants with sharp cutter. Avoid excessive Watering and dumpy condition which are favourable to spread of fungal
RICE BLAST.prevention: Blight disease - TOMATOES mulch fields or plants with dry weeds to reduce contact with dumpy soil .Crop rotation

ROT.current_treatment: 1.CHANGE PLANTING MATERIAL,AFTER THREE(3)CULTIVATION 2.ROTATE PLANT WITH LEGUMINEOUS AND CEREAL CROPS. 3.TRY OTHER IMPROVEED VARIETIES SUCH AS AMPOM,SIKA BANKYE .....
ROT.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or planting materials. Avoid planting in waterlogged areas and ensure good drainage. Rotate crops with non-susceptible plants for 3-4 years. Remove and burn all crop residues after harvest. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during high-risk periods such as rainy seasons. Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation.

MANAGEMENT ISSUE.current_treatment: 1. Good land preparation 2. Hoeing 3. Use Nicogan 2 weeks after sowing at 80 mls 120 mls to 15 litres of water under moist soil condition.
MANAGEMENT ISSUE.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

POOR SPACING.current_treatment: 1. Plant at 1 meter by 1 meter (recommended planting distance) in the next season 2. Plant improved varieties such as Bankyehemaa etc. For better yield
POOR SPACING.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

COLLAR ROT.current_treatment: Collar rot - TOMATO 1. Create channels to drain excess water from the farm 2. Drench root zone of plants with mankocide ( mancozeb + copper ) 30g per 15 litres water at 2 weeks interval for 2 times.
COLLAR ROT.prevention: Collar rot - TOMATO 1. Avoid planting tomato in waterlogged areas. 2. Rotate crops to non host crops such as maize for 2-3 years.

CALCIUM DEFICIENCY.current_treatment: PRACTICE TIMELY WEEDING TO AVOID WEEDS COMPETING WITH THE CROP WITH NUTRIENT AND APPLY ANY FERTILIZER THAT CONTAIN CALCIUM EG. SINGLE SUPER PHOSPHATE
CALCIUM DEFICIENCY.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

WEEDS.current_treatment: 1.(a) WEED REGULARLY AT LEAST TWICE A SEASON (b) WEED TWO WEEKS AFTER CROP SPROUTS, BEFORE THE STRIGA REACHES THE ROOT OF THE CROP (c) WEED AGAIN SIX TO EIGHT WEEKS BEFORE SPROUTING 2. PLANT RESISTANT VARIETIES OF COWPEA,GROUNDNUT AND SORGHUM TO CONTROL THE WEED. 3. APPLY NITROGEN FERTILIZER TO INCREASE TH NUTRIENT IN THE SOIL
WEEDS.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

UNKNOWN.current_treatment: Rotate crops with non-host crops, acquire Seeds at the approved seed sales points.
UNKNOWN.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

BUSHFIRE.current_treatment: The farm should be cleared of weeds before the drought period commence Fire belt should be constructed around the farm to prevent fire from entering the farm
BUSHFIRE.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

TERMITE.current_treatment: Harvest early and gather the stalks and burn after harvesting.
TERMITE.prevention: Look for ant -Hill around the farm and destroy it.

MICRONUTRIENT DEFICIENCY.current_treatment: 1. Spray a solution of 70g of poly feed and 15l of water on the whole plant,should be applied in the late afternoon when temperature is low in the green house. Repeat after 10 days.
MICRONUTRIENT DEFICIENCY.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

THRIPS.current_treatment: Apply Acetastar at a rate of 40mls/ knapsack.
THRIPS.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

TOMATO MOSAIC VIRUS.current_treatment: ROGUE AND BURN AFFECTED INDIVIDUAL PLANT.
TOMATO MOSAIC VIRUS.prevention: Plant virus-resistant varieties where available. Use certified virus-free planting materials. Control insect vectors through regular monitoring and appropriate management. Remove infected plants immediately to prevent spread. Avoid planting near infected fields. Use reflective mulches to repel insect vectors.

VERTICILLIUM WILT.current_treatment: Verticilum wilt - TOMATOES Remove and burn the diseased plants.
VERTICILLIUM WILT.prevention: Verticillium wilt - Avoid planting in fields with a history of disease attack. Use certified seeds and discard affected seedlings.

WILT.current_treatment: Rotate your crops any time you observe wilt in your farm, by planting cassava, maize and plantain instead of repeatedly planting Tomato. Pull all tomato plants that wiltand burn or burry. Practice land fallowing for a longer period, do not plant tomato on the same land until the fallow period elapses.
WILT.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting materials. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops for at least 2-3 years. Maintain good field sanitation by removing and destroying infected plant debris. Monitor fields regularly for early detection of symptoms. Plant resistant varieties where available. Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.

WITCHES BROOM.current_treatment: CASSAVA uproot and burn the crop materials from the field.
WITCHES BROOM.prevention: - CASSAVA change variety by planting improved variety such as bankyihimma, ampong, esambankyi.
